subtitle: Literature-to-Execution Workspace
tagline: From papers to artifacts — one disciplined workflow.
description: Manage your entire research portfolio from a single dashboard. Import literature from Undermind or Zotero, track Vibe Coding & Vibe Research execution steps, generate daily and weekly reports — all with local web AI assistance, no API keys required.

quickstart.title: Export from Undermind → Import
whatYouDo: Run a search on app.undermind.ai for your research topic
Download the results CSV from the Undermind dashboard
whatYouGet: Papers with relevance scores, topic match, and abstracts
Auto-indexed and tagged in your project's literature tab
quickstart.code:
# Undermind CSV columns (auto-detected)
Title, Authors, Year, Venue, Citations,
Relevance Summary, Topic Match Score,
Abstract, URL

quickstart.title: Export from Zotero → Import
whatYouDo: Select papers in your Zotero library and export as CSV
Upload the CSV file to your project's literature importer
whatYouGet: Papers with metadata, abstracts, DOIs, and notes
Deduplication against existing papers in the project
quickstart.code:
# Zotero CSV columns (auto-detected)
Key, Item Type, Title, Author,
Abstract Note, Publication Year,
DOI, Url, Date Added

quickstart.title: Vibe Research Loop
whatYouDo: Tag papers → write notes → build project outline
Use any browser-based AI (Gemini, ChatGPT) to refine
whatYouGet: Structured overview with 12 section types (definition, pipeline, risks…)
quickstart.code:
# Vibe Research cycle
tag papers → annotate → outline
→ structured overview → milestones
→ weekly report

quickstart.title: Vibe Coding Loop
whatYouDo: Cycle step statuses: todo → doing → done → blocked
whatYouGet: Execution steps with progress bars and blocker alerts
Daily reports with activity logs and artifact registry
quickstart.code:
# Vibe Coding cycle
plan → implement → test → artifact
→ execution steps → daily report
→ kanban board

literature.title: Undermind + Zotero Import
literature.desc: CSV import with auto-format detection. Relevance scores, abstracts, DOIs parsed automatically.

literature.title: Tag & Annotate Papers
literature.desc: Multi-tag papers, write per-paper notes, pin important references. Search across all projects.

literature.title: Citation Tracking
literature.desc: Track citation counts, publication venues, and year distributions across your literature corpus.

literature.title: Cross-Project Literature
literature.desc: Global literature index spans all projects. Papers linked to multiple projects with shared tags.

execution.title: Dual VC/VR Progress
execution.desc: Separate Vibe Coding and Vibe Research checklists with independent progress tracking.

execution.title: Status Cycling
execution.desc: Click to cycle: todo → doing → done → blocked. Inline editing for all step fields.

execution.title: Milestone Timeline
execution.desc: Milestones with deadlines, acceptance criteria, and status badges. Kanban board for task flow.

execution.title: Artifact Registry
execution.desc: Track code repos, datasets, papers, demos, and reports with color-coded type badges.

execution.title: AI Template Generation
execution.desc: Download template, fill with any LLM in your browser, upload. No API keys ever needed.

reporting.title: Daily Reports
reporting.desc: Auto-generated from real data: step changes, log entries, artifact additions, literature imports.

reporting.title: Weekly Reports
reporting.desc: Portfolio dashboard with progress deltas, blocker counts, milestone status, and literature growth.

reporting.title: Home Dashboard
reporting.desc: Latest report timestamps on the homepage. Priority sorting, RAG status, and filter controls.

reporting.title: Markdown Export
reporting.desc: All reports saved as Markdown files. Export, version-control, or share with collaborators.

faq.q: Do I need any API keys?
faq.a: No. LabPilot is entirely API-key-free. You use browser-based AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude) to fill templates externally, then upload the result. No provider configuration is needed.

faq.q: Can I use only Zotero (no Undermind)?
faq.a: Yes. Both Undermind and Zotero CSV imports are fully supported and independent. You can use one, both, or neither — manual paper entry is also available.

They are two complementary disciplines. Vibe Coding focuses on implementation tasks (code, experiments, artifacts). Vibe Research focuses on literature, theory, and writing. Each has its own checklist, progress bar, and execution steps.

faq.q: How do I keep projects consistent?
faq.a: Use the template workflow: download the YAML template, fill all 12 sections with an LLM, upload the JSON. This generates a structured overview, execution steps, milestones, and kanban items — ensuring every project has the same rich structure.

faq.q: How do I structure execution steps?
faq.a: Steps are auto-generated from your template but fully editable. Each step has a title, description, status (todo/doing/done/blocked), and optional acceptance criteria. They sync to the progress bars on the dashboard.
No. All data is stored as flat files (YAML, JSON, Markdown, JSONL) in the project directory. Easy to backup, version-control, and inspect with standard tools.
Yes — LabPilot is designed for self-hosting. Clone the repo, install Python and Node dependencies, and run the FastAPI server. Works on any Linux/macOS machine with Python 3.11+ and Node.js 20+.

faq.q: What tech stack does it use?
faq.a: FastAPI + Pydantic V2 backend, React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + TailwindCSS v4 frontend. Data stored as flat YAML/JSON/Markdown files — no database required.
Daily and weekly reports compile real project data: checklist deltas, punch-in logs, artifact additions, literature imports, and milestone changes into structured Markdown documents.

A JSON document with 12 typed sections: definition, motivation, scope, key questions, concepts, pipeline, evaluation, deliverables, milestones, risks, literature entry points, and next actions. Each section renders as a rich card in the UI.
